finding_results.evidence: manual_rescue/chapters/chapter_09.md:53 (retained: 'calibrated through a personality matrix'), manual_rescue/chapters/chapter_09.md:67 (replaced: 'configured to respond to'), manual_rescue/chapters/chapter_09.md:81 (replaced: 'gauged to what the moment required'), manual_rescue/chapters/chapter_09.md:99 (replaced: 'weighted for maximum emotional reciprocity')
finding_results.notes: Retained the most directly technical instance at line 53, where the system's personality matrix is described as scoring affect and adjusting latency — this is literal parameter calibration. Replaced the other three with distinct words from Simone's UX/design vocabulary: 'configured' (line 67, AI preference profile), 'gauged' (line 81, Simone's performative warmth under pressure), 'weighted' (line 99, AI sentiment response). Each substitute appears once as a replacement. Pre-existing uses of 'tuned' (line 111) and 'adjusted' (line 79) were avoided to prevent local clustering.

summary: Single finding addressed via three targeted lexical substitutions. 'Calibrated' reduced from 4 to 1 instance. The retained instance is the most technically specific (personality matrix scoring at line 53). Replacements draw on three distinct words from Simone's UX vocabulary, each used once, avoiding new tics. No structural, continuity, or dialogue-register changes were needed — the chapter's prose, voice, and scene architecture remain intact.
